records.ety: [F. dgorger, earlier desgorger; pref. d-, des- (L. dis-) + gorge. See Gorge.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disgorged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disgorging.]

defs: To eject or discharge by the throat and mouth; to vomit; to pour forth or throw out with violence, as if from the mouth; to discharge violently or in great quantities from a confined place.
quotes: This mountain when it rageth, . . . casteth forth huge stones, disgorgeth brimstone.
They loudly laughed To see his heaving breast disgorge the briny draught.

defs: To give up unwillingly as what one has wrongfully seized and appropriated; to make restitution of; to surrender; as, he was compelled to disgorge his ill-gotten gains.
To vomit forth what anything contains; to discharge; to make restitution.
quotes: See where it flows, disgorging at seven mouths Into the sea.
